title: Nvidia market share in China falls to less than 60% Chinese chip makers deliver 1.65 million AI GPUs as the government pushes data centers to use domestic chips
summary: Nvidia's market share in China drops to 55% as local chip makers gain ground, delivering 1.65 million AI GPUs.
insight: Government support boosts domestic chip makers at Nvidia's expense in China.

title: Save $100 on the gaming powerhouse AMD Ryzen 5 7600X3D, now $246 on Amazon budget-friendly X3D processor with 96MB cache, low power draw, and excellent gaming performance
summary: The Ryzen 5 7600X3D offers exceptional gaming performance at a budget-friendly price. This processor boasts a 96MB cache and low power draw, making it an attractive option for gamers. It delivers X3D-level performance at a lower cost than higher-end models.
insight: Budget gamers get high-performance X3D-level processing without breaking the bank.

title: Nvidia App adds 'Auto Shader Compilation' for faster load times in games beta feature automatically recompiles shaders in the background after every driver update
summary: The Nvidia App now auto-compiles shaders in background after driver updates. This saves gamers time, especially in blockbuster titles. Initial compile still required after new install.
insight: Faster game load times through automatic shader recompilation

title: DRAM prices predicted to jump 63% in Q2, NAND up to 75% follows 95% jumps in Q1, Trendforce says AI server demand keeps supply tight
summary: DRAM contract prices are predicted to rise by 58-63% in Q2 2026, while NAND Flash prices will jump 70-75% QoQ. This significant increase is attributed to tight supply due to strong demand for AI servers. The prices saw a 95% jump in Q1.
insight: Tight AI server demand exacerbates memory price volatility.

title: Arm to sell its new AGI CPU in China 'we would expect the demand for this product to be just as strong in China as it is in the rest of the world'
summary: Arm's 136-core Neoverse V3 AGI processor is eligible for sale in China, with expected strong demand.
insight: China presents a significant market opportunity for Arm's new AGI processor.

title: PC makers report surging prices across different components increasing costs are going beyond memory chip and processors, now affecting PCBs, plastic materials, and more
summary: PC makers face rising costs from suppliers across various components, including processors, memory chips, PCBs, and plastics. Price hikes are affecting multiple parts of the manufacturing process. This is causing increased expenses for PC manufacturers.
insight: Supply chain disruptions lead to widespread price increases in PC components.

title: Gigabyte X870E Aorus Xtreme AI Top Motherboard Review: Solid flagship, but the X3D version is what you want today
summary: The Gigabyte X870E Aorus Xtreme AI Top delivers solid performance and premium features, but is pricey and outdated.
insight: Newer refresh boards like the X3D version outshine the X870E.

title: Microsoft issues emergency update for Windows 11 fixes broken March preview update rollout from last week
summary: Microsoft released an emergency update for Windows 11 to address install errors caused by a previous preview update. The new update replaces the problematic KB5079391 rollout, aiming to resolve widespread issues. Users can now safely install the latest Windows 11 updates.
insight: Fixing critical Windows update errors prevents data loss and downtime.

title: Oracle believed to have cut 10,000 positions across multiple divisions as mass layoffs begin to fuel AI investments company is reportedly reducing headcount to fund data centers
summary: Oracle reportedly cut over 10,000 positions to fund AI investments, amid billions spent on AI infrastructure.
insight: Layoffs fund AI ambitions, potentially long-term financial instability follows.

title: Pay $611 for 64GB of Corsair Vengeance RAM and Samsung's 2TB PCIe Gen 5 9100 Pro when paired with a Gigabyte X870 Auros Elite motherboard and Corsair Frame 5000D case matching colors for a pure white build
summary: This build features 64GB of DDR5 RAM, a 2TB SSD, and a Gigabyte motherboard, all paired with a Corsair case in a pure white design.
insight: Future-proof gaming performance with high-capacity storage and memory.

title: Hacker charged for stealing $53 million in crypto, faces up to 30 years in prison Uranium Finance thief spent $2 million of illicit funds on Magic: The Gathering, $1 million on Pok mon cards
summary: Jonathan Spalletta is charged with stealing $53.3 million in cryptocurrency from Uranium Finance. The theft led to the shut down of the finance platform. He faces up to 30 years in prison for computer fraud and money laundering.
insight: Cybercrime poses significant financial risks to global digital economies.

title: PC enthusiast finds relic Nvidia 3D Vision 2 glasses for $2.99 PC gaming artifact from 2011 cost $149 new, was once Nvidia's 'vision' for the future of gaming
summary: A PC enthusiast bought sealed Nvidia 3D Vision 2 glasses for $2.99, a significant discount from its original $149 price.
insight: Nvidia's 2011 3D vision technology seems largely obsolete now, abandoned.

title: Apple turns 50 one of the world's biggest tech companies started with hobbyist computers
summary: Apple, one of the world's largest tech companies, celebrates its 50th anniversary. Founded by Steve Jobs, Steve Wozniak, and Ronald Wayne, the company started as a hobbyist computer manufacturer. Its early roots led to innovative products and global success.
insight: Hobbyist roots fueled innovation and shaped Apple's path to greatness.

title: Entry-level Intel Core 3 304 Wildcat Lake processor details and benchmarks surface
summary: Intel's Wildcat Lake Core Series 3 CPUs have been revealed with penta-core processor details, including the entry-level Intel Core 3 304, which has seen some benchmarking. The Wildcat Lake SKUs have varying levels of SIPP support and GPU turbo clocks, outperforming their predecessors. Further information has been obtained from a post by Jaykihn on X.
insight: Wildcat Lake CPUs offer significant GPU turbo clock improvements over predecessors.

title: Digi IX25 A rugged 5G RedCap/eMBB industrial cellular router
summary: Digi IX25 is a rugged 5G industrial cellular router for critical and enterprise applications, offering cellular networking, edge computing, and secure connectivity in a single device.
insight: Rugged 5G router simplifies deployment in harsh environments efficiently.

title: Raspberry Pi 4 3GB launched for $83.75, further price increases announced across the board for 4GB+ RAM hardware
summary: Raspberry Pi 4 with 3GB RAM is now available for $83.75, with no changes to its specifications. A price increase has been announced for the 4GB+ RAM hardware due to the rising cost of LPDDR4 DRAM.
insight: Rising DRAM costs lead to sudden Raspberry Pi price hikes.

title: Artemis II with four crew aboard lifts off for Moon
summary: The Artemis II mission successfully launched with an Orion space capsule carrying four astronauts. The crew is on a historic mission to the Moon, paving the way for future human exploration. The Space Flight System and rocket performed flawlessly.
insight: Pioneering human lunar mission sets stage for long-term space presence.

title: NASA contractors supply an array of sensing tech for Artemis II
summary: NASA contractors are supplying various sensing technologies for the Artemis II mission. These technologies include sensor fusion, display systems, and radiation detectors. The sensors will help monitor the crew's health and safety.
insight: Advanced sensing tech ensures crew safety during deep space missions.

title: Assembly of 59 Best of Sensors 2026 finalists announced
summary: The 59 finalists for the Best of Sensors 2026 awards have been announced. The winners will be revealed on May 6 at Sensors Converge 2026 in Santa Clara. The event will take place at the Santa Clara Convention Center.
insight: Innovation in sensors technology is key to driving industry advancements.

title: Sony's upcoming handheld could outperform Xbox Series S with next-gen upscaling
summary: Sony's rumored handheld, codenamed Canis, may outperform Xbox Series S with a faster graphics chip. The device is expected to complement the PlayStation 6, releasing late 2027 at the earliest. KeplerL2 made the claim recently.
insight: Sony's handheld could bridge the gap with next-gen console performance.

title: Google offers researchers early access to Willow quantum processor
summary: Google offers researchers early access to its Willow quantum processor through a selective Early Access Program. This program invites researchers to design and propose experiments that push the boundaries of current hardware capabilities. Participation has a firm deadline of May 15.
insight: Accelerating quantum research and innovation through early hardware access.

title: US government hires BlackSky to build next-gen AI surveillance satellites for Earth and beyond
summary: The US government has selected BlackSky to build advanced AI surveillance satellites.
insight: Enhanced US space surveillance capabilities boost national security and competitiveness.

title: Linux gaming levels up as CachyOS beats Windows 11 in head-to-head tests
summary: Linux-based CachyOS outperforms Windows 11 in head-to-head gaming tests, using identical hardware.
insight: Linux gaming can rival Windows' performance on high-end hardware configurations.

title: AirPods Max 2 reviewed: premium sound, top-tier ANC, same high price
summary: AirPods Max 2 offer improved sound quality and top-tier noise cancellation, powered by the H2 chip, while maintaining a high price.
insight: Premium audio and ANC advancements justify the expensive upgrade cost.

title: Google Drive can now detect ransomware and roll back your files
summary: Google Drive now detects and rolls back ransomware attacks on files using AI-powered tools.
insight: Enhanced security protects against devastating ransomware attacks on personal files.

title: A paralyzed musician is using a brain implant to create music
summary: Buckwalter, paralyzed from the chest down, creates music with a brain implant. He underwent a craniotomy and six Blackrock Neurotech chips were implanted in his motor cortex. This allowed him to compose music through brain signals.
insight: Brain-computer interfaces can restore creative abilities in individuals with paralysis.

title: Anthropic accidentally exposed Claude Code source, raising security concerns
summary: Anthropic accidentally exposed Claude's code source through a JavaScript debugging map. The incident occurred in version 2.1.88 of the @anthropic-ai/claude-code package on npm. This has raised concerns over security and intellectual property.
insight: Exposure of source code can compromise AI model security

title: Crimson Desert hits 4 million sales despite rocky launch
summary: Crimson Desert, a highly anticipated game, received mixed reviews and a poor Steam rating, leading to a decline in developer Pearl Abyss' shares.
insight: Mixed reviews and user feedback impact game developer's stock value significantly.

title: Oracle layoffs could reach 30,000 as company doubles down on AI
summary: Oracle may lay off 20,000 to 30,000 employees due to AI focus.
insight: Mass layoffs signal shift towards AI-driven workforce automation.

title: Tesla admits that remote humans can sometimes take control of its robotaxis
summary: Tesla revealed that remote humans may take control of its robotaxis occasionally. This disclosure comes from a response to an investigation into the use of remote assistance operators in autonomous vehicles. The investigation aims to ensure the safety and accountability of autonomous vehicle companies.
insight: Trust in autonomous vehicles is compromised by human intervention.

title: You can finally change your Gmail address without losing everything
summary: Gmail users in the US can change usernames without losing access to services.
insight: Preserves online identity and continuity for users changing email usernames.

title: Hypervisor-based cracks are breaking Denuvo protections in hours
summary: Denuvo's anti-tamper technology has been cracked by a new virtualization-based method, breaking the company's aura of invincibility.
insight: Rapid cracks undermine game developers' revenue and intellectual property protection.

title: Iran threatens attacks on Nvidia, Microsoft, Intel, and other US tech firms in the Middle East
summary: Iran's IRGC threatens to target US tech firms in Middle East, including Nvidia, Microsoft, and Intel, over their involvement in military technology.
insight: US tech interests in Middle East are at risk of Iranian retaliation.

title: AI can clone open-source software in minutes, and that's a problem
summary: AI tools can rapidly clone open-source software, creating proprietary versions with legal distinctions.
insight: Bending copyright law and open-source principles with AI-powered cloning threatens innovation.
